headline: SK lays off nearly 1,000 workers at Georgia plant amid cooling automaker EV plans
summary: The company said Friday marked the last working day for 958 plant employees, about 37% of its workforce, according to a Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification, or WARN, notice filed by human resources chief Chuck Moore.  Impacted workers will be paid through May 6.

headline: Ford Recalls Millions of Cars. The Stock Is Down.
summary: Ford Motor  is facing another round of recalls.  The company reported almost 2.4 million recalls in six separate actions on Tuesday, according to data posted on the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration website.  The latest batch of recalls were mainly related to issues with backup cameras and windshield wipers.

raw.headline: Ford Recalls Millions of Cars. The Stock Is Down.

headline: GM and Ford: Bank of America Names 2 Top Auto Stocks to Buy for 2026
summary: The changeover of Presidential Administrations always brings changes of policy priorities, and those can ripple outward. Sometimes, it’s impossible to know where the ripples will end, and sometimes, it’s easy. Last month, one of the easier ripples made itself felt. The second Trump Administration took office promising to roll back large parts of the Biden regulatory regime, including difficult findings on greenhouse gases and automobile fuel economy. headline: Wary Shoppers Drag Consumer Discretionary Earnings to 2020 Low
summary: Only 56% of companies in the S&P 500 consumer discretionary sector beat GAAP earnings estimates in the fourth quarter, according to Bloomberg Intelligence data.  Consumers remain “choiceful” as inflation lingers, while tariffs hurt gross margins in the back half of 2025, said Steven Shemesh, a consumer discretionary equity analyst at RBC Capital Markets Corp.
